name: Peñaranda de Bracamonte and Cantaracillo Loop
name_original: Peñaranda de Bracamonte y Cantaracillo — circular
description: This moderate 6.6-mile (10.6 km) circular hike near Peñaranda de Bracamonte explores historical towns and scenic cereal fields.
region_intro: Hiking around Peñaranda De Bracamonte offers diverse landscapes, historical routes, and tranquil natural features. The region is characterized by scenic cereal fields that provide a picturesque backdrop for many walking routes. A significant natural attraction is the Azud de Riolobos, a weir or lake offering a tranquil landscape. The flat terrain of the plain provides accessible trails suitable for various fitness levels. Best hiking trails around Peñaranda De Bracamonte The most popular hiking route is Peñaranda de Bracamonte and Cantaracillo Loop, a 6.6 miles (10.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 43 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores both historical towns and surrounding cereal fields. Another top favourite among local hikers is Return to Azud de Riolobos from El Campo de Peñaranda, a moderate 10.0 miles (16.0 km) path. This trail leads to the tranquil Azud de Riolobos, offering opportunities for bird watching. Local hikers also love the La Charca loop from Macotera, a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 27 minutes. Hiking around Peñaranda De Bracamonte is defined by scenic cereal fields, flat plains, and the tranquil Azud de Riolobos. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate excursions. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 20 hikers have used komoot to explore Peñaranda De Bracamonte's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Peñaranda De Bracamonte?
A: There are currently 8 hiking routes documented around Peñaranda De Bracamonte, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: What kind of landscapes will I encounter on hikes in Peñaranda De Bracamonte?
A: The region is characterized by picturesque cereal fields and flat plains, providing a tranquil backdrop for your walks. A notable natural feature is the Azud de Riolobos, a weir or lake that offers a beautiful and calm environment, ideal for bird watching.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options?
A: Yes, the flat terrain around Peñaranda De Bracamonte makes many trails accessible. While most routes are moderate, there is at least one easy trail suitable for beginners or families looking for a relaxed walk. The overall network offers options for various ability levels.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Peñaranda De Bracamonte are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282796959/penaranda-de-bracamonte-and-cantaracillo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaranda de Bracamonte and Cantaracillo Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 10.6 km circular route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20141628" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Peñaranda de Bracamonte&lt;/a&gt; is another popular circular option.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I explore while hiking?
A: Peñaranda de Bracamonte is rich in history. You can explore historical towns and routes like the Carlos V Route, which passes through the area. The region also lies on the ancient Cañada de las Merinas drovers' road. Notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5558454" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Miguel of Peñaranda de Bracamonte&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6063447" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of the Assumption&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Peñaranda De Bracamonte?
A: Given the region's characteristics of cereal fields and flat plains, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with mild temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summers can be warm, while winters are typically mild but can be chilly.

Q: Are there any trails that feature water bodies or opportunities for bird watching?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282873292/return-to-azud-de-riolobos-from-el-campo-de-penaranda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to Azud de Riolobos from El Campo de Peñaranda&lt;/a&gt; trail leads directly to the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5337563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riolobos Weir&lt;/a&gt;, which is an excellent spot for bird watching and enjoying a peaceful waterside environment.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Peñaranda De Bracamonte?
A: The komoot community highly rates the hiking experience in Peñaranda De Bracamonte, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the tranquility of the routes, and the opportunity to explore both natural beauty and historical towns.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: While most routes are moderate, some offer a longer distance. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282873292/return-to-azud-de-riolobos-from-el-campo-de-penaranda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to Azud de Riolobos from El Campo de Peñaranda&lt;/a&gt;, at 16 km, is one of the longer moderate options, providing a good challenge for those seeking a more extended excursion through the region's scenic areas.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through or near other villages?
A: Yes, some routes connect Peñaranda De Bracamonte with nearby villages. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282796959/penaranda-de-bracamonte-and-cantaracillo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaranda de Bracamonte and Cantaracillo Loop&lt;/a&gt; explores both Peñaranda and the historical town of Cantaracillo, offering a blend of natural and cultural exploration.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the surrounding agricultural land?
A: Many trails in the region traverse the scenic cereal fields that define the landscape around Peñaranda De Bracamonte. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282796959/penaranda-de-bracamonte-and-cantaracillo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaranda de Bracamonte and Cantaracillo Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer extensive views of these picturesque agricultural areas.
tips: The Renaissance-style parish church of San Miguel has existed since the mid-15th century as a church-fortress. Its interior was almost completely destroyed in 1971 after a fire.

Church of Nuestra Señora de La Asunción is a Mudejar-style church, renovated in the 16th century. From the first construction phase (12th - 13th centuries), only part of the central apse remains. Its tower stands out, called "La Buena Moza", visible from several kilometers.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hike?
faqs.answer: This circular trail is classified as moderate. It requires a good level of physical fitness, though the paths are generally easily accessible.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Peñaranda de Bracamonte y Cantaracillo — circular is a loop trail, meaning you will end up back at your starting point.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the trailhead are not provided, the route starts and ends in Peñaranda de Bracamonte, suggesting you can likely find parking within the town or near the route's beginning.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail generally features easily accessible paths. The broader Peñaranda de Bracamonte area is characterized by cereal fields and other natural spaces, so you can expect a mix of these landscapes.

faqs.question: What are the notable landmarks or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll pass through Cantaracillo, which features historical sites such as the Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción. In Peñaranda de Bracamonte, you can see the Church of San Miguel and the town's significant historical and artistic elements, including its porticoed squares. The Church of the Assumption is also a highlight.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed throughout the year, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for hiking in the region. Always check local weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other official trails, including the Ruta Teresiana, Ruta del Sureste, Ruta de Carlos V, Calle Cristo, Travesía Carretera de Madrid, and Plaza de Agustín Martínez Soler.

faqs.question: Is the Peñaranda de Bracamonte y Cantaracillo — circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there are no specific restrictions mentioned, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near historical sites, to ensure their safety and respect for the local environment.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access this trail.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: For this moderate hike, we recommend bringing appropriate footwear, sufficient water, snacks, sun protection, and a charged phone. Given the trail's length and duration, comfortable clothing is also essential.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation nearby?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Peñaranda de Bracamonte, a town with historical significance. You can expect to find various facilities, including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options, within the town.

waypoints.name: Church of San Miguel of Peñaranda de Bracamonte
